mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals expanding consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.02 or 20-day forward returns declining in months with positive RSAFS growth in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 8% signals a tightening labor market that supports household income and confidence, boosting equities over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in the 12 months following the first UNRATE < 8% trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based manufacturing and construction expansion, which together lift equity valuations through higher capacity utilization and real estate demand over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined INDPRO+HOUST momentum factor is < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, further declines are infeasible and signal economic weakness, making any drop in FEDFUNDS (or a sustained low level) negative for S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days as it implies a worsening growth outlook.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or if a long-short portfolio based on FEDFUNDS declines underperforms cash.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% prompts Fed tightening expectations, which discount future equity cash flows more heavily and cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If average 20-day returns after CPI YoY > 2.5% are not negative (one-tailed t-test p < 0.10), or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market that raises aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following PAYEMS releases is positive less than 55% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly above zero at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a synchronized expansion in both final demand and manufacturing output, which jointly boost corporate earnings and equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined signal from GDPC1 and INDPRO has a moving-window Rank IC below 0.02 over 3 years, or if the interaction term in a VECM is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 600 thousand units in the post-crisis environment signals a self-sustaining housing recovery that feeds construction employment, consumer spending, and bank balance sheets, lifting equities over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of months when HOUST > 600, or if the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).
